lesson.menu_title: Milestone Review -- From Maceio to Seoul
lesson.menu_desc: Milestone Review: one third of the program. Put it all together in a full Seoul simulation in three acts -- Act 1, the welcome reception (introduce yourself, small talk, ask about the agenda); Act 2, the round table (give your opinion, agree and disagree, ask 'Would you mind repeating that?', handle a change of plan); Act 3, the dinner debrief (narrate the day, recommend with should and could, close with going to and will). No new grammar -- you activate everything from Lessons 1 to 14. Review words: delegation, conference, agenda, colleague, clarify, priority, recommend, debrief. -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 15 -- Milestone Review: From Maceio to Seoul
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 15 -- Milestone Review: From Maceio to Seoul

listenings.text: Hello, this is a message to myself, a small progress check before Seoul. Fifteen lessons ago, I was afraid to say one word in English. Today I can introduce myself, I can talk about my role at the university, and I can tell a story about a past event. I still make mistakes with the tenses, and sometimes I need a moment to find the word. That is fine. Last week I spoke for three minutes without switching to Portuguese, and that was a first for me. My priority now is to keep practicing every day and to feel calm when I speak. I am going to be ready for the conference. I speak English, and I am improving.

Good morning, and welcome to the International University Leadership Conference here in Seoul. Here is a short announcement about today's agenda for the visiting delegation. The welcome reception starts at nine in the main hall, so please arrive a few minutes early. At ten, we begin the first round table, where each institution presents its work and shares an opinion on the main topic. If you do not understand a question, do not worry; you can always ask the speaker to repeat it. There is one small change of plan: the interpreter will not be here this morning, so the introductions will be in English. In the evening, there is a dinner for the whole delegation at seven. We recommend that you rest a little before it. Thank you, and enjoy the conference.

items.situation: You arrive at the welcome reception and meet a colleague from another university. Introduce yourself: your name, your role, and your institution.
tips: Hello, I am Maria Clara. I am a professor and I lead the delegation from my university.
Present simple for who you are and what you do.

items.situation: The colleague asks: "How was your trip from Brazil?" Answer with one thing that happened on the way.
tips: It was long, but it was fine. While we were waiting for the flight, we reviewed the agenda.
Past simple + past continuous for the trip.

items.situation: At the round table, someone gives an opinion you do not share. Disagree politely and give your view.
tips: I see your point, but in my opinion we should start with the documents.
I disagree = your view, with respect.

items.situation: A speaker talks too fast and you miss the question. Ask them to repeat it, politely.
tips: Sorry, would you mind repeating that?
Would you mind + -ing = a polite request.

items.situation: The interpreter will not be here, so you must handle the introduction yourself. Say you will take care of it.
tips: No problem. I will handle the introduction.
will for a decision you make now.

items.situation: At the dinner debrief, a colleague asks what you recommend for tomorrow. Give one recommendation.
tips: I recommend that we arrive early, and we could visit the old palace after the sessions.
